reasoning: A has the most genuinely distinct five: it explicitly snaps a different assumption per concept (The Recursion exhibits the institution's own live failures via autophagy; The Wet Lab makes the visitor the recorded data; Things That Almost Were exhibits counterfactual absence with empty plinths; The Half-Life Wing puts a reclassification timer on each piece; The Confessional Exchange makes a failure the price of admission), so no two share a conceptual space. B is vivid and emotionally well-rendered but clusters: Vault of Withheld Verdicts, Participation Floor, and Erosion Garden retread the late-verdict, you-are-the-exhibit, and slow-decay ideas that overlap each other and A's stronger versions. C is the thinnest and most compressed, with Hall of Almosts and Broken Rehearsal echoing near-miss and participation tropes; its one standout, Museum of Successful Failures (moral failure as flawless execution), is not enough to lift the set above A or B.
